title: dactylorhiza incarnata var. borealis
intro: Dactylorhiza incarnata var. borealis, also known as the Northern marsh orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid that belongs to the Orchidaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza incarnata var. borealis include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and processing the roots and leaves to be used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
